title: Pure additive-only config merge function
action: Create src/config/merge.rs (declared via `pub mod merge;` in src/config/mod.rs) with a pure function `pub fn merge_missing_fields(existing_json: &str, default_json: &str) -> Result<String, MergeError>`. Parse both arguments as `serde_json::Value` and require each is a JSON object (`Value::Object`), else return `MergeError::NotAnObject`. For every key present in `default_json`'s object but ABSENT from `existing_json`'s object, insert the default's value for that key into the result. Every key already present in `existing_json` is NEVER modified, regardless of its value or type (per D6 -- user values are sacred). Every key present in `existing_json` but ABSENT from `default_json` (an orphaned field from an older config shape) is preserved untouched in the result, never dropped, never warned about (D6 -- no rename-mapping in v1). Serialize the merged object back to a JSON string via `serde_json::to_string_pretty` (does not need to byte-match ensure_config's own hand-formatted style -- just needs to be valid parseable JSON). No file I/O, no network in this cell. Name the four required unit tests exactly: `merge_adds_missing_field_with_default_value`, `merge_preserves_existing_user_value_unchanged`, `merge_preserves_orphaned_field_not_in_default`, `merge_errors_when_existing_is_not_a_json_object`.

truths: A field present in default_json but absent from existing_json is added to the result with default_json's value
A field present in both, with different values, keeps existing_json's value unchanged in the result
A field present only in existing_json (not in default_json) is preserved untouched in the result
A non-object existing_json (e.g. a JSON array or a bare string) returns MergeError::NotAnObject rather than panicking or silently proceeding

artifacts.substantive: exports merge_missing_fields and MergeError with the 4 named unit tests, no TODO stubs

prohibitions: No file I/O or network calls in this cell

verify: test -f src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'pub mod merge' src/config/mod.rs && grep -q 'fn merge_adds_missing_field_with_default_value' src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'fn merge_preserves_existing_user_value_unchanged' src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'fn merge_preserves_orphaned_field_not_in_default' src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'fn merge_errors_when_existing_is_not_a_json_object' src/config/merge.rs && cargo test --quiet config

trace.outcome: Added pure additive-only merge_missing_fields(existing_json, default_json) in new src/config/merge.rs, wired via pub mod merge in src/config/mod.rs; seeds missing default fields, never touches existing user values, preserves orphaned fields, fails closed with MergeError::NotAnObject on non-object input
